county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Miami County formed 1855 as an original Kansas Territory county. County seat is Paola. Named for the Miami people (originally from Ohio/Indiana). Border war violence damaged early records. Eastern Kansas border county with both pro-slavery and Free State factions.
county_metadata.research_tips: Miami County suffered record losses from border war raids (1856-1861) along the Missouri border. Register of Deeds in Paola holds surviving and post-war records. The Miami County Historical Society and Museum in Paola has local genealogical collections. KSHS holds territorial-era documents for Miami County. BLM GLO land patents fill gaps in damaged land records. The county had both pro-slavery and Free State factions. Church records from Methodist and Baptist congregations from the 1850s. Probate Court records from 1855 (with some gaps). Paola newspapers at KSHS. FamilySearch has microfilmed surviving Miami County records. Osage Mission (St. Paul) records may document Native American families.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Miami County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Paola

source_profiles.source_name: Miami County Deed Books -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.repository: Miami County Register of Deeds, Paola
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original deed books for Miami County from 1855; grantor/grantee indexes; mortgages; liens; Register of Deeds office maintains land records
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival from 1855; deed books are substantially complete

source_profiles.repository: Miami County Probate Court, Paola

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Mitchell County formed 1867, organized 1870. County seat is Beloit. Named for Colonel William D. Mitchell, killed in Civil War. North-central Kansas. Solomon River valley. Czech, Scandinavian, and German immigrant communities. Glen Elder Dam area.
county_metadata.research_tips: Mitchell County Register of Deeds in Beloit holds land records from the 1870s. The Mitchell County Historical Society and Museum in Beloit has local genealogical collections. BLM GLO land patents document homestead entries. Scandinavian, German, and Czech immigrant communities left church records from the 1870s-1880s. Probate Court records from the 1870s. Beloit newspapers at KSHS. FamilySearch has microfilmed some Mitchell County records. The Solomon River valley was the primary settlement corridor. Glen Elder Dam construction affected some communities; check KSHS for relocation records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Mitchell County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Beloit

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Montgomery County formed 1867 from Wilson County. County seat is Independence. Named for General Richard Montgomery, killed in American Revolution. Independence and Coffeyville were significant commercial centers. Dalton Gang's failed Coffeyville raid (1892). Oil and gas production. African American Exoduster settlement.
county_metadata.research_tips: Montgomery County Register of Deeds in Independence holds land records from 1867. Independence and Coffeyville were significant commercial centers. The Independence Historical Museum and Art Center has local collections. BLM GLO land patents. The county attracted African American settlers (Exodusters). Oil and gas production from the early 1900s. Church records from Methodist, Baptist, and AME congregations. Probate Court records from 1867. Independence and Coffeyville newspapers at KSHS. FamilySearch has microfilmed Montgomery County records. The Dalton Gang's failed Coffeyville raid (1892) is documented at KSHS and local museums. District Court naturalization records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Montgomery County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Independence

county_metadata.parent_county: Original (unorganized territory -- originally Wise County, renamed 1859)
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Morris County formed 1855 as Wise County (renamed Morris 1859). County seat is Council Grove. Named for Thomas Morris, US Senator from Ohio. Council Grove was the last outfitting point on the Santa Fe Trail. Kaw (Kansa) Nation council ground and Methodist mission. Kaw Mission State Historic Site.
county_metadata.research_tips: Morris County (originally Wise County until 1859) Register of Deeds in Council Grove holds land records from 1855. Council Grove was the last outfitting point on the Santa Fe Trail -- the Kaw (Kansa) Nation council ground and mission records are at KSHS and NARA. The Morris County Historical Society in Council Grove has local collections. Kaw Mission State Historic Site documents Native American education. BLM GLO land patents. Church records from Methodist and Presbyterian congregations from the 1850s. Probate Court records from 1855. Council Grove Republican newspaper at KSHS. FamilySearch has microfilmed Morris County records. When searching early records, use 'Wise County' for the pre-1859 period.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Morris County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Council Grove

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Morton County formed 1886. County seat is Elkhart. Named for Oliver H.P.T. Morton, governor of Indiana. Far-southwestern corner of Kansas, last county settled. Cimarron National Grassland. Extreme Dust Bowl devastation. Sparsely populated.
county_metadata.research_tips: Morton County Register of Deeds in Elkhart holds land records from 1886. The far-southwestern corner of Kansas, last settled. The Morton County Historical Society and Museum in Elkhart has local collections. BLM GLO land patents are essential for tracing homesteaders. Probate Court records from 1886. Church records from pioneer congregations are sparse due to late settlement. Elkhart newspapers at KSHS. FamilySearch has limited microfilmed records. Dust Bowl devastation was extreme in Morton County. Cimarron National Grassland occupies much of the county. Mexican American agricultural workers settled from the mid-20th century.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Morton County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Elkhart

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Nemaha County formed 1855 as an original Kansas Territory county. County seat is Seneca. Named for the Nemaha River. Strong German Catholic heritage. Borders the Kickapoo Reservation. Northeastern Kansas agricultural county.
county_metadata.research_tips: Nemaha County Register of Deeds in Seneca holds land records from 1855. The county has a strong German Catholic heritage, particularly around Seneca, Baileyville, and Centralia. The Nemaha County Historical Society in Seneca has local genealogical collections. Catholic parish registers from the 1850s are exceptionally detailed. BLM GLO land patents. Irish and German immigrant communities. Probate Court records from 1855. Seneca newspapers at KSHS. FamilySearch has microfilmed Nemaha County records. District Court naturalization records document immigrant arrivals. The county borders the Kickapoo Reservation -- tribal records at NARA Kansas City.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Nemaha County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Seneca

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Neosho County formed 1855 as an original Kansas Territory county. County seat is Erie. Named for the Neosho River. Chanute was a major Santa Fe Railroad division point. Osage Mission (later St. Paul) was a Catholic mission to the Osage Nation. Early oil production.
county_metadata.research_tips: Neosho County Register of Deeds in Erie holds land records from 1855. Chanute was a major Santa Fe Railroad division point -- railroad records at KSHS. The Neosho County Historical Society in Erie has local genealogical collections. BLM GLO land patents. Osage Mission (later St. Paul) was a Catholic mission to the Osage Nation -- mission records at KSHS and Catholic diocesan archives. Church records from various denominations from the 1850s. Probate Court records from 1855. Erie and Chanute newspapers at KSHS. FamilySearch has microfilmed Neosho County records. The county had early oil production; petroleum records may document families.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Neosho County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Erie

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Ness County formed 1867, organized 1880. County seat is Ness City. Named for Corporal Noah V. Ness, killed in Civil War. Western Kansas county. County seat contested between Ness City and Sidney. German-Russian settlers. Dust Bowl effects.
county_metadata.research_tips: Ness County Register of Deeds in Ness City holds land records from the 1880s (practical settlement). The Ness County Historical Society has local collections. BLM GLO land patents are critical for tracing homesteaders in this western Kansas county. Probate Court records from the 1880s. Church records from pioneer Catholic and Methodist congregations. Ness City newspapers at KSHS. FamilySearch has limited microfilmed records. German-Russian settlers left church records at local parishes. Dust Bowl effects caused outmigration. The county seat was contested between Ness City and Sidney; check both town records at KSHS.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Ness County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Ness City

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Norton County formed 1867, organized 1872. County seat is Norton. Named for Captain Orloff Norton, killed in Civil War. Northwestern Kansas county bordering Nebraska. Sebelius Reservoir. Czech, German, and Scandinavian settlers.
county_metadata.research_tips: Norton County Register of Deeds in Norton holds land records from the 1870s. The Norton County Historical Society and Museum has local genealogical collections. BLM GLO land patents document homestead entries. German, Czech, and Scandinavian settlers left church records from the 1870s-1880s. Probate Court records from the 1870s. Norton newspapers at KSHS. FamilySearch has microfilmed some Norton County records. The county borders Nebraska -- cross-border family research may be necessary. Keith Sebelius Lake construction affected some communities.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Norton County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Norton

county_metadata.parent_county: Original (unorganized territory -- originally Weller County, renamed 1859)
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Osage County formed 1855 as Weller County (renamed Osage 1859). County seat is Lyndon. Named for the Osage people. Part of the Osage diminished reserve. Melvern and Pomona Lakes constructed in the 1960s. Free State connections.
county_metadata.research_tips: Osage County (originally Weller County until 1859) Register of Deeds in Lyndon holds land records from 1855. The county was part of the Osage Nation diminished reserve -- NARA holds Osage agency records. The Osage County Historical Society in Lyndon has local collections. BLM GLO land patents. Church records from Methodist and Catholic congregations from the 1850s. Probate Court records from 1855. Lyndon newspapers at KSHS. FamilySearch has microfilmed Osage County records. When searching early records, use 'Weller County' for the pre-1859 period. Melvern Lake and Pomona Lake construction displaced some communities. The county had ties to John Brown's Free State activities.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Osage County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Lyndon

source_profiles.source_name: Osage County Deed Books -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.repository: Osage County Register of Deeds, Lyndon
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original deed books for Osage County from 1855; grantor/grantee indexes; mortgages; liens; Register of Deeds office maintains land records
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival from 1855; deed books are substantially complete

source_profiles.source_name: Osage County Probate Court Records
source_profiles.repository: Osage County Probate Court, Lyndon
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, inventories, guardianships, and estate settlements for Osage County; Probate Court has jurisdiction over estate matters

source_profiles.source_name: Osage County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.repository: NARA / FamilySearch / Ancestry
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1860-1950 for Osage County; Kansas Territory census 1855 and 1859 may include Osage County entries at KSHS; state census 1865, 1875, 1885, 1895, 1905, 1915, 1925 at KSHS

source_profiles.source_name: Osage County Federal Land Patents -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal land patents (homestead, preemption, timber culture, cash entry) for Osage County; Kansas is a public land state and GLO patents document original federal land transfers to private ownership

source_profiles.source_name: Osage County Territorial-Era Records -- KSHS
source_profiles.repository: Kansas State Historical Society, Topeka
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Kansas territorial records for Osage County including territorial census (1855, 1859), land claims, Free State and pro-slavery legislature records, correspondence, and early settler petitions held at KSHS

source_profiles.source_name: Osage County Marriage Records -- Probate Court / KSHS / FamilySearch
source_profiles.repository: Osage County Probate Court, Lyndon / Kansas State Historical Society, Topeka
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and records for Osage County from 1855; Probate Court issued marriage licenses in Kansas; KSHS holds some county marriage records on microfilm; FamilySearch has indexed Kansas marriages; state vital records registration began 1911

source_profiles.source_name: Osage County Naturalization Records -- District Court
source_profiles.repository: Osage County District Court, Lyndon
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Declarations of intention and petitions for naturalization filed in Osage County District Court; document immigrant arrivals and origins; held at the courthouse or transferred to KSHS
